fields.description:
## Why
Multi-backend reconcilers (ticket systems beyond Jira) are planned. Today the Jira/Atlassian assumption is threaded across ~24 reconciler files with no vendor-adapter seam, and the 55-module reconciler is a flat namespace. These are one design problem: the sub-package boundary IS the vendor-adapter seam. (O5 + S5 â€” verified; combined per maintainer.)

Citations: O5 â€” Jira coupling across `_engine/rebar_reconciler/` (`acli*`, `adf.py`, `jira_fields.py`, `outbound_fields.py`, differ/apply cluster; `outbound_fields.py` touched 2026-07-06 for one status mapping). S5 â€” 55 flat `.py` files, no sub-packaging.

## What
DESIGN/ADR FIRST (do this now): author an ADR mapping (a) the Jira-coupled modules, (b) the operations that must become vendor-neutral (issue CRUD, field mapping via outbound_fields/adf, the acli transport), (c) the target sub-package layout separating a backend-neutral core (differs/apply/dispatch/store) from an `adapters/jira/` vendor layer, and (d) the interface a second backend would implement. THEN execute the sub-packaging around that seam, preserving import paths. Do NOT build a speculative full adapter abstraction ahead of the ADR â€” the ADR decides the seam.

## Scope
Reconciler structure + vendor seam. Sequence the code move AFTER the reconciler function-extraction and git-adapter tickets so modules move once. This is the enabler for the future second-backend initiative.

## Plan-review remediation â€” preservation mechanism + phased execution
The naive "package `__init__` re-exports" mechanism is insufficient for two verified reasons; both are addressed by a **loader-safe, patch-safe, phased** execution:

1. **Module-qualified attribute access (e.g. `acli_subprocess._run_acli`, patched module-qualified in tests).** A package `__init__` re-export exposes a *symbol* but not the old *module path*, and re-exporting into a shim module creates a patch-binding pitfall (a test patching the old path would not affect the canonical module's binding). Therefore, per moved module, ALL references are migrated to the new canonical path in the same change â€” internal importers, every test patch target, and any `importlib`/string-qualified reference â€” so there is exactly one binding site. A module whose reference/patch surface is too broad to migrate safely in this change is NOT moved in Phase 1; it is inventoried in the ADR for Phase 2.
2. **The file-location dynamic loader (`_loader.lazy_load` / `__main__._load_sibling_keyed`) resolves siblings by `_PACKAGE_DIR / filename`.** It loads only backend-neutral machinery (`mode.py`, `_advisory_lock.py`, `_ref_lock.py`, `mutation.py`, `_errors.py`), which STAY at the package root in Phase 1, so the loader is untouched and unbroken. The Jira vendor modules (`acli*`, `adf.py`, `jira_fields.py`, `outbound_fields.py`) are imported normally (not dynamically loaded), so relocating them cannot break the loader. Any Phase-2 relocation of a dynamically-loaded module must also update the loader to discover the new sub-package dir.

**Phase 1 (this change):** author the ADR; create the `adapters/jira/` vendor package; physically relocate the vendor modules that can be migrated safely (all refs updated, full suite green); update `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt` + `.github/module-size-ceilings.txt` PATHS for any moved over-cap file. **Phase 2 (follow-up ticket, linked `discovered_from`):** relocate the remaining vendor + core modules per the ADR layout, updating the loader and the broad test-reference surface, and retire any transitional shims.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ADR authored: Jira-coupling inventory + vendor-neutral operation list + target sub-package layout + second-backend interface sketch (design done NOW)
- [ ] `adapters/jira/` vendor package established as the seam; the safe subset of vendor modules relocated there, with import paths preserved (all internal/test/dynamic references resolve; full suite green)
- [ ] Module-size allowlist + ceilings PATHS updated for any relocated over-cap file
- [ ] Adding a second backend is documented in the ADR as "implement `adapters/<name>/` against the seam"
- [ ] Remaining full-reorg scope captured as a Phase-2 follow-up ticket linked `discovered_from`
